steps.title: Factor the numerator
steps.content: To simplify the fraction, factor $x^2 + 6x - 16$. We need two numbers that multiply to $-16$ and add to $6$. Those numbers are $8$ and $-2$, giving us: $$x^2 + 6x - 16 = (x + 8)(x - 2)$$

steps.title: Cancel the common factor and identify coefficients
steps.content: Substitute the factored form into the original expression: $$\frac{(x + 8)(x - 2)}{x + 8} = x - 2$$ for all $x \neq -8$. Comparing to the form $ax + d$, we identify $a = 1$ and $d = -2$. Therefore, $a + d = 1 + (-2) = -1$. That matches Choice C — $-1$.
